title: lkparq: claude-based parquet transformation of TxDb from Gencode v49
text: experiment evaluate approach TxDb production decoupled AnnotationDbi based serializations information Gencode GTFs Parquet. code transformed Gencode GTF parquet inst/python sources package. code generated prompting Anthropic Claude Opus 4.5 March 2026.
code: library(GenomicRanges) library(arrow) library(dplyr) library(lkparq) parqloc = system.file("gc49", package="lkparq") gtf <- GTFParquet(parqloc) print(gtf) ## GTFParquet object ##   Path: /private/var/folders/yw/gfhgh7k565v9w83x_k764wbc0000gp/T/RtmplBznwD/temp_libpath82ef7fcad40d/lkparq/gc49  ##   Genome: GRCh38  ##   Available tables: ##     [x] genes ##     [x] transcripts ##     [x] exons ##     [x] cds ##     [x] features ##     [x] metadata ##   Metadata: ##     description: evidence-based annotation of the human genome (GRCh38), version 49 (Ensembl 115) ##     provider: GENCODE ##     contact: gencode-help@ebi.ac.uk ##     format: gtf ##     date: 2025-07-08 ##     genome: GRCh38 # View GTF metadata (version, date, provider) gtf_metadata(gtf) ##                                                                        description  ## "evidence-based annotation of the human genome (GRCh38), version 49 (Ensembl 115)"  ##                                                                           provider  ##                                                                          "GENCODE"  ##                                                                            contact  ##                                                           "gencode-help@ebi.ac.uk"  ##                                                                             format  ##                                                                              "gtf"  ##                                                                               date  ##                                                                       "2025-07-08"  ##                                                                             genome  ##                                                                           "GRCh38"

what: Get ALL genes with FULL attributes

title: lkparq: claude-based parquet transformation of TxDb from Gencode v49
text: Level 1: verified loci (experimental evidence) Level 2: manually annotated loci Level 3: automatically annotated loci
code: verified_genes <- genes(gtf, filter = list(level = 1))  # Filter by source (HAVANA = manual, ENSEMBL = automatic) manual_genes <- genes(gtf, filter = list(source = "HAVANA"))  # Filter by tags # Common tags: basic, CCDS, overlaps_pseudogene, etc. # Note: tags is a semicolon-separated string, so use grep for complex filters all_genes <- genes(gtf) basic_genes <- all_genes[grep("basic", mcols(all_genes)$tags)]  # Combine filters chr1_protein_coding <- genes(gtf, filter = list(   chrom = "chr1",   gene_type = "protein_coding" ))

title: lkparq: claude-based parquet transformation of TxDb from Gencode v49
code: # Transcripts include transcript_type, transcript_name, support level, etc. tx <- transcripts(gtf) head(mcols(tx)) ## DataFrame with 6 rows and 11 columns ##                 transcript_name transcript_type           gene_id   gene_name ##                     <character>     <character>       <character> <character> ## ENST00000832824    DDX11L16-260          lncRNA ENSG00000290825.2    DDX11L16 ## ENST00000832825    DDX11L16-261          lncRNA ENSG00000290825.2    DDX11L16 ## ENST00000832826    DDX11L16-262          lncRNA ENSG00000290825.2    DDX11L16 ## ENST00000832827    DDX11L16-263          lncRNA ENSG00000290825.2    DDX11L16 ## ENST00000832828    DDX11L16-264          lncRNA ENSG00000290825.2    DDX11L16 ## ENST00000832829    DDX11L16-265          lncRNA ENSG00000290825.2    DDX11L16 ##                      source     level         tags transcript_support_level ##                 <character> <integer>  <character>              <character> ## ENST00000832824      HAVANA        NA       TAGENE                       NA ## ENST00000832825      HAVANA        NA       TAGENE                       NA ## ENST00000832826      HAVANA        NA       TAGENE                       NA ## ENST00000832827      HAVANA        NA       TAGENE                       NA ## ENST00000832828      HAVANA        NA basic;TAGENE                       NA ## ENST00000832829      HAVANA        NA       TAGENE                       NA ##                 havana_transcript      ccdsid  protein_id ##                       <character> <character> <character> ## ENST00000832824                NA          NA          NA ## ENST00000832825                NA          NA          NA ## ENST00000832826                NA          NA          NA ## ENST00000832827                NA          NA          NA ## ENST00000832828                NA          NA          NA ## ENST00000832829                NA          NA          NA #   transcript_name transcript_type gene_id gene_name source level tags #   transcript_support_level havana_transcript ccdsid protein_id  # See transcript types transcript_types(gtf) ##  ##                           artifact                          IG_C_gene  ##                                 19                                 23  ##                    IG_C_pseudogene                          IG_D_gene  ##                                  9                                 37  ##                          IG_J_gene                    IG_J_pseudogene  ##                                 18                                  3  ##                      IG_pseudogene                          IG_V_gene  ##                                  1                                146  ##                    IG_V_pseudogene                             lncRNA  ##                                187                             189152  ##                              miRNA                           misc_RNA  ##                               1879                               2207  ##                            Mt_rRNA                            Mt_tRNA  ##                                  2                                 22  ##                     non_stop_decay            nonsense_mediated_decay  ##                                105                              21949  ##               processed_pseudogene               processed_transcript  ##                               9488                                 12  ##                     protein_coding     protein_coding_CDS_not_defined  ##                             211446                              26573  ##                 protein_coding_LoF                    retained_intron  ##                                 74                              34239  ##                           ribozyme                               rRNA  ##                                  8                                 47  ##                    rRNA_pseudogene                             scaRNA  ##                                497                                 49  ##                             snoRNA                              snRNA  ##                                942                               1901  ##                               sRNA                                TEC  ##                                  5                               1108  ##                          TR_C_gene                          TR_D_gene  ##                                  6                                  5  ##                          TR_J_gene                    TR_J_pseudogene  ##                                 79                                  4  ##                          TR_V_gene                    TR_V_pseudogene  ##                                107                                 33  ##   transcribed_processed_pseudogene     transcribed_unitary_pseudogene  ##                               1149                                201  ## transcribed_unprocessed_pseudogene    translated_processed_pseudogene  ##                               1589                                  2  ##                 unitary_pseudogene             unprocessed_pseudogene  ##                                 89                               1949  ##                          vault_RNA  ##                                  4 # Filter by transcript type coding_tx <- transcripts(gtf, filter = list(transcript_type = "protein_coding"))  # Filter by transcript support level (TSL) # TSL1 = all splice junctions supported by RNA-seq # TSL2 = best supporting transcript is flagged as suspect # TSL3 = not all splice junctions supported # TSL4/5 = poor support high_confidence_tx <- transcripts(gtf, filter = list(transcript_support_level = "1"))  # Find transcripts with CCDS IDs (highly curated) all_tx <- transcripts(gtf) ccds_tx <- all_tx[!is.na(mcols(all_tx)$ccdsid)] length(ccds_tx) ## [1] 95584

title: lkparq: claude-based parquet transformation of TxDb from Gencode v49

                  <integer>       <character>       <character> <character> ## ENSE00004248723          NA ENST00000832824.1 ENSG00000290825.2      HAVANA ## ENSE00004248721          NA ENST00000832825.1 ENSG00000290825.2      HAVANA ## ENSE00004248726          NA ENST00000832826.1 ENSG00000290825.2      HAVANA ## ENSE00004248710          NA ENST00000832827.1 ENSG00000290825.2      HAVANA ## ENSE00004248702          NA ENST00000832828.1 ENSG00000290825.2      HAVANA ## ENSE00004248719          NA ENST00000832829.1 ENSG00000290825.2      HAVANA ##                     level         tags ##                 <integer>  <character> ## ENSE00004248723        NA       TAGENE ## ENSE00004248721        NA       TAGENE ## ENSE00004248726        NA       TAGENE ## ENSE00004248710        NA       TAGENE ## ENSE00004248702        NA basic;TAGENE ## ENSE00004248719        NA       TAGENE # Exons for a specific gene brca1_exons <- exons(gtf, filter = list(gene_id_stripped = "ENSG00000012048"))  # Exons grouped by transcript exons_by_tx <- exonsBy(gtf, by = "tx") length(exons_by_tx)  # Number of transcripts ##   ENSE00003504066     chr7 127589485-127589594      + |        <NA> ##   ENSE00003678978     chr7 127590066-127590137      + |        <NA> ##   ENSE00003676786     chr7 127590963-127591088      + |        <NA> ##   ENSE00000882271     chr7 127591213-127591700      + |        <NA> ##                   gene_id_stripped ##                        <character> ##   ENSE00001872691  ENSG00000004059 ##   ENSE00003494180  ENSG00000004059 ##   ENSE00003504066  ENSG00000004059 ##   ENSE00003678978  ENSG00000004059 ##   ENSE00003676786  ENSG00000004059 ##   ENSE00000882271  ENSG00000004059 ##   ------- ##   seqinfo: 25 sequences from GRCh38 genome; no seqlengths # Exons grouped by gene exons_by_gene <- exonsBy(gtf, by = "gene")

code: # 5' UTRs utr5 <- utrs(gtf, type = "5prime")  # 3' UTRs utr3 <- utrs(gtf, type = "3prime")  # Start codons start_codons <- codons(gtf, type = "start")  # Stop codons stop_codons <- codons(gtf, type = "stop")

Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  ##   1.000   1.000   1.000   6.448   6.000 522.000 # Genes with most isoforms head(sort(tx_counts, decreasing = TRUE), 20) ## ENSG00000304176 ENSG00000304309 ENSG00000249859 ENSG00000227888 ENSG00000255717  ##             522             448             426             411             363  ## ENSG00000181722 ENSG00000291178 ENSG00000310527 ENSG00000223482 ENSG00000272168  ##             360             353             331             327             326  ## ENSG00000093167 ENSG00000215386 ENSG00000290549 ENSG00000228315 ENSG00000293616  ##             321             317             310             289             289  ## ENSG00000310526 ENSG00000231721 ENSG00000291158 ENSG00000148180 ENSG00000291154  ##             285             284             276             274             270

title: lkparq: claude-based parquet transformation of TxDb from Gencode v49
code: # Find genes in a region region <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1e6, 2e6)) genes_in_query <- genes_in_region(gtf, region)  # Find transcripts in a region tx_in_query <- transcripts_in_region(gtf, region) # Get promoter regions (now with gene_type!) promoters <- promoters(genes(gtf), upstream = 2000, downstream = 200) # promoters still has all mcols from genes head(mcols(promoters)$gene_type) ## [1] "lncRNA"                             "transcribed_unprocessed_pseudogene" ## [3] "lncRNA"                             "transcribed_unprocessed_pseudogene" ## [5] "miRNA"                              "lncRNA" # Filter for protein-coding promoters pc_promoters <- promoters[mcols(promoters)$gene_type == "protein_coding"] head(pc_promoters) ## GRanges object with 6 ranges and 6 metadata columns: ##                   seqnames        ranges strand |   gene_name      gene_type ##                      <Rle>     <IRanges>  <Rle> | <character>    <character> ##   ENSG00000186092     chr1   63419-65618      + |       OR4F5 protein_coding ##   ENSG00000284733     chr1 451479-453678      - |      OR4F29 protein_coding ##   ENSG00000284662     chr1 686455-688654      - |      OR4F16 protein_coding ##   ENSG00000187634     chr1 921923-924122      + |      SAMD11 protein_coding ##   ENSG00000188976     chr1 960515-962714      - |       NOC2L protein_coding ##   ENSG00000187961     chr1 958576-960775      + |      KLHL17 protein_coding ##                        source     level        tags           havana_gene ##                   <character> <integer> <character>           <character> ##   ENSG00000186092      HAVANA      <NA>        <NA>  OTTHUMG00000001094.4 ##   ENSG00000284733      HAVANA      <NA>        <NA>  OTTHUMG00000002860.3 ##   ENSG00000284662      HAVANA      <NA>        <NA>  OTTHUMG00000002581.3 ##   ENSG00000187634      HAVANA      <NA>        <NA> OTTHUMG00000040719.11 ##   ENSG00000188976      HAVANA      <NA>        <NA>  OTTHUMG00000040720.2 ##   ENSG00000187961      HAVANA      <NA>        <NA>  OTTHUMG00000040721.8 ##   ------- ##   seqinfo: 25 sequences from GRCh38 genome; no seqlengths

title: lkparq: claude-based parquet transformation of TxDb from Gencode v49
text: Use filters reduce data loaded genes(gtf, filter = list(chrom = “chr1”)) # Fast genes(gtf)[seqnames(genes(gtf)) == “chr1”] # Slower (loads first) Select columns need genes(gtf, columns = c(“gene_name”, “gene_type”)) Use versioned IDs need genes(gtf, use_versioned_ids = TRUE) # ENSG00000290825.2 genes(gtf, use_versioned_ids = FALSE) # ENSG00000290825 (default) large operations, consider working Parquet directly
code: library(arrow)     genes_tbl <- open_dataset("parquet_output/genes.parquet")     genes_tbl |> filter(gene_type == "protein_coding") |> collect()

title: parquet for txdb
text: package heavily negotiated Claude chat Opus 4.5 extended March 2026. takes advantage works many people involved Bioconductor packages GenomicState txdbmaker AnnotationDbi main purpose expose GTF content directly possible using methods GenomicFeatures. back end present parquet.

title: GTFParquet class — GTFParquet-class
text: S4 class accessing GTF annotations stored Parquet format. Unlike TxDb, preserves GTF attributes (gene_type, gene_name, transcript_support_level, tags, etc.)

title: GTFParquet class — GTFParquet-class
code: # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' genome(x)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' seqinfo(x)

title: GTFParquet class — GTFParquet-class
text: x GTFParquet object.

title: GTFParquet class — GTFParquet-class
text: Seqinfo object containing chromosome names   genome build.

title: GTFParquet class — GTFParquet-class
text: GTFParquet objects created GTFParquet constructor function directory Parquet files generated gtf_to_parquet.py. class implements methods GenomicFeatures generics including genes, transcripts, exons, cds, exonsBy, cdsBy, transcriptsBy. methods support filter argument efficient querying (e.g., filter = list(gene_type = "protein_coding")).

title: GTFParquet class — GTFParquet-class
text: path Character. Path Parquet directory. files List. Paths individual Parquet files. available Logical vector. files present. is_partitioned Logical. Whether genes partitioned chromosome. .genome Character. Reference genome build (e.g., "GRCh38").

title: GTFParquet class — GTFParquet-class
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create from Parquet directory gtf <- GTFParquet(system.file("gc49", package="lkparq"))  # Extract genes with full attributes gr <- genes(gtf) mcols(gr)  # gene_name, gene_type, level, tags, etc.  # Filter by gene type pc <- genes(gtf, filter = list(gene_type = "protein_coding")) } # }

what: Extract genomic features from a GTFParquet object — genes
title: Extract genomic features from a GTFParquet object — genes
text: Methods extract genomic features GTFParquet object GRanges. Unlike TxDb methods, preserve GTF attributes metadata columns.

title: Extract genomic features from a GTFParquet object — genes
code: # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' genes(x, columns=NULL, filter=NULL, use_versioned_ids=FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' transcripts(x, columns=NULL, filter=NULL, use_versioned_ids=FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' exons(x, columns=NULL, filter=NULL, use_versioned_ids=FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' cds(x, columns=NULL, filter=NULL)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' transcripts(x, columns = NULL, filter = NULL, use_versioned_ids = FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' exons(x, columns = NULL, filter = NULL, use_versioned_ids = FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' cds(x, columns = NULL, filter = NULL)

title: Extract genomic features from a GTFParquet object — genes
text: x GTFParquet object. columns Character vector columns include mcols. NULL (default), includes available attribute columns. genes: gene_name, gene_type, source, level, tags, havana_gene. transcripts: transcript_name, transcript_type, gene_id, gene_name, transcript_support_level, ccdsid, protein_id. filter Optional named list filtering features. Names column names, values vectors acceptable values. Example: filter = list(gene_type = "protein_coding", chrom = "chr1") use_versioned_ids Logical. TRUE, use full versioned IDs (e.g., ENSG00000141510.18). FALSE (default), use stripped IDs (e.g., ENSG00000141510).

title: Extract genomic features from a GTFParquet object — genes
text: GRanges object : Feature IDs names Genomic coordinates (seqnames, ranges, strand) Genome build seqinfo (e.g., "GRCh38") Rich metadata mcols

title: Extract genomic features from a GTFParquet object — genes
text: methods return GRanges objects feature IDs names rich metadata columns original GTF file. filter argument enables efficient server-side filtering Arrow/Parquet predicate pushdown, can dramatically improve performance compared subsetting loading. Available filter columns include: chrom: Chromosome name gene_type: Gene biotype (e.g., "protein_coding", "lncRNA") transcript_type: Transcript biotype level: Annotation confidence (1=verified, 2=manual, 3=automatic) source: Annotation source ("HAVANA", "ENSEMBL")

title: Extract genomic features from a GTFParquet object — genes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gtf <- GTFParquet(system.file("gc49", package="lkparq"))  # Extract all genes with full attributes gr <- genes(gtf) mcols(gr)  # gene_name, gene_type, level, tags, source, havana_gene  # Filter by gene type pc <- genes(gtf, filter = list(gene_type = "protein_coding")) lnc <- genes(gtf, filter = list(gene_type = "lncRNA"))  # Combine filters pc_chr1 <- genes(gtf, filter = list(gene_type = "protein_coding", chrom = "chr1"))  # Select specific columns only gr <- genes(gtf, columns = c("gene_name", "gene_type"))  # Use versioned IDs gr <- genes(gtf, use_versioned_ids = TRUE) names(gr)[1]  # "ENSG00000141510.18"  # Transcripts with support level tx <- transcripts(gtf) high_conf <- tx[mcols(tx)$transcript_support_level == "1"]  # Exons ex <- exons(gtf, filter = list(chrom = "chr1"))  # CDS with protein IDs cds_gr <- cds(gtf) mcols(cds_gr)$protein_id } # }

code: gtf_metadata(x)

title: Get GTF metadata — gtf_metadata
text: x GTFParquet object.

title: Get GTF metadata — gtf_metadata
text: named character vector metadata key-value pairs.

title: Get GTF metadata — gtf_metadata
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gtf <- GTFParquet(system.file("gc49", package="lkparq")) gtf_metadata(gtf) #      provider         format           date         genome  #      "GENCODE"          "gtf"   "2025-07-08"       "GRCh38" } # }

what: Find features overlapping a genomic region — region-queries
title: Find features overlapping a genomic region — region-queries
text: Efficient region queries use chromosome-based filtering computing overlaps.

title: Find features overlapping a genomic region — region-queries
code: genes_in_region(x, region, ...)  transcripts_in_region(x, region, ...)

title: Find features overlapping a genomic region — region-queries
text: x GTFParquet object. region GRanges object specifying query region(s). ... Additional arguments passed genes() transcripts().

title: Find features overlapping a genomic region — region-queries
text: GRanges object containing features   overlap query region.

title: Find features overlapping a genomic region — region-queries
text: functions first filter chromosome (using Parquet predicate pushdown efficiency), compute overlaps using findOverlaps.

title: Find features overlapping a genomic region — region-queries
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gtf <- GTFParquet(system.file("gc49", package="lkparq"))  # Define a query region region <- GRanges("chr1", IRanges(1000000, 2000000))  # Find overlapping genes genes_in_region(gtf, region)  # Find overlapping transcripts (protein-coding only) transcripts_in_region(gtf, region,                        filter = list(transcript_type = "protein_coding")) } # }

what: Extract and group genomic features from a GTFParquet object — transcriptsBy
title: Extract and group genomic features from a GTFParquet object — transcriptsBy
text: Generic functions extract genomic features given type grouped based another type genomic feature. methods extend GenomicFeatures generics GTFParquet objects.

title: Extract and group genomic features from a GTFParquet object — transcriptsBy
code: # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' transcriptsBy(x, by="gene", filter=NULL)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' exonsBy(x, by=c("tx", "gene"), filter=NULL)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' cdsBy(x, by=c("tx", "gene"), filter=NULL)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' cdsBy(x, by = c("tx", "gene"), filter = NULL)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' transcriptsBy(x, by = "gene", filter = NULL)

title: Extract and group genomic features from a GTFParquet object — transcriptsBy
text: x GTFParquet object. One "gene", "tx" (transcript). Determines grouping. transcriptsBy, "gene" currently supported. filter Optional named list filtering features grouping. Names column names (e.g., gene_type, chrom), values vectors acceptable values. Example: filter = list(gene_type = "protein_coding", chrom = "chr1")

title: Extract and group genomic features from a GTFParquet object — transcriptsBy
text: GRangesList object. names list elements IDs grouping features (gene IDs transcript IDs). GTFParquet objects, names use stripped (unversioned) IDs default (e.g., ENSG00000141510 rather ENSG00000141510.18).

title: Extract and group genomic features from a GTFParquet object — transcriptsBy
text: functions return GRangesList object ranges within elements ordered according following rule: using exonsBy cdsBy = "tx", returned exons CDS ordered ascending exon number transcript, , position transcript. cases, ranges ordered chromosome, strand, start, end values. Unlike TxDb methods, GTFParquet methods preserve rich metadata columns including transcript_name, transcript_type, exon_number, protein_id, frame. filter argument allows efficient server-side filtering data loaded R, can dramatically improve performance large annotation files.

title: Extract and group genomic features from a GTFParquet object — transcriptsBy
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gtf <- GTFParquet(system.file("gc49", package="lkparq"))  # Exons grouped by transcript (sorted by exon_number) ebt <- exonsBy(gtf, by = "tx") ebt[[1]]  # Exons for first transcript  # Exons grouped by gene ebg <- exonsBy(gtf, by = "gene")  # CDS grouped by transcript cbt <- cdsBy(gtf, by = "tx")  # Transcripts grouped by gene tbg <- transcriptsBy(gtf, by = "gene")  # Filter to protein-coding only pc_exons <- exonsBy(gtf, by = "tx",                      filter = list(gene_type = "protein_coding"))  # Filter by chromosome chr1_cds <- cdsBy(gtf, by = "tx", filter = list(chrom = "chr1")) } # }

what: Extract UTR and codon features from a GTFParquet object — utrs
title: Extract UTR and codon features from a GTFParquet object — utrs
text: Functions extract UTR (untranslated region) codon features GTFParquet object. features stored features.parquet file generated gtf_to_parquet.py.

title: Extract UTR and codon features from a GTFParquet object — utrs
code: utrs(x, type = "both", filter = NULL)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' utrs(x, type = c("both", "5prime", "3prime"), filter = NULL)  codons(x, type = "both", filter = NULL)  # S4 method for class 'GTFParquet' codons(x, type = c("both", "start", "stop"), filter = NULL)

title: Extract UTR and codon features from a GTFParquet object — utrs
text: x GTFParquet object. type utrs: one "", "5prime", "3prime". codons: one "", "start", "stop". filter Optional named list filtering features.

title: Extract UTR and codon features from a GTFParquet object — utrs
text: GRanges object metadata columns   including feature_type, transcript_id, gene_id.

title: Extract UTR and codon features from a GTFParquet object — utrs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gtf <- GTFParquet(system.file("gc49", package="lkparq"))  # 5' UTRs utr5 <- utrs(gtf, type = "5prime")  # 3' UTRs utr3 <- utrs(gtf, type = "3prime")  # Start codons start <- codons(gtf, type = "start")  # Stop codons stop <- codons(gtf, type = "stop") } # }
